text.title: YouTube Niche and Video Formats
text.summary: Niche formula, validation criteria, content pillars, and video format selection by goal.

rule.statement: Define the channel niche as: [Your expertise] + [Specific audience] + [Content format]. Validate all three axes before publishing.
rule.rationale: YouTube's algorithm rewards consistent niche signals. Generalist channels struggle to build a loyal subscriber base because the algorithm cannot identify who to recommend the channel to.

example.note: SaaS marketing + indie founders + tutorials and case studies. Specific expertise, identifiable audience, consistent format.
Marketing tips for everyone. No expertise specificity, no audience definition, no format constraint — the algorithm has no signal to optimize.

rule.statement: Validate a niche on four dimensions before committing: demand, differentiation, sustainability (100+ video ideas), and monetization path.
rule.rationale: A niche with demand but no monetization path produces views without revenue. A niche with a product but no demand produces content nobody watches.

list.item: Demand: are people actively searching for this on YouTube?
Competition: can you differentiate from existing channels in this niche?
Sustainability: can you generate 100+ video ideas without forcing it?
Monetization: is there a product, service, or course to sell to this audience?

rule.statement: Choose 3-4 content pillars and map every video to at least one pillar. Do not publish videos outside the pillars.
rule.rationale: Pillar discipline maintains the algorithm signal and subscriber expectations. Off-pillar videos fragment the audience and reduce subscriber conversion rate.

rule.statement: Prioritize search-intent formats (tutorials, list videos) during the first 6-12 months. Switch to discovery formats (opinion, commentary) only after establishing a subscriber base.
rule.rationale: Search-intent videos rank for queries indefinitely, compounding traffic. Discovery content depends on algorithmic push, which requires watch-time history to earn.

list.item: Tutorials (10-20 min): search traffic, authority building
List videos (8-15 min): easy to produce, highly searchable
Case studies (15-30 min): trust building, conversion
Interviews (30-60 min): audience borrowing, relationship building
Shorts (<60 sec): top-of-funnel discovery, supplement long-form
